llm_diagnosis: Abel's Markov blanket has no node for foreign-economy growth contribution, so 'second largest economy' and 'biggest contributor to expansion' did not map onto inflation/GDP/unemployment and defaulted to neutral. text: and given our highly educated population canada has the capacity to innovate in green technologieswhile the magnitude of the transition itself provides a strong motivation for such innovation
llm_diagnosis: The sentence is framed in terms of human capital and green innovation capacity, concepts Abel's blanket treats as structural rather than cyclical, so it flattened a confidence/expansion signal into neutral. Abel missed that capacity-to-innovate language is implicitly growth-positive in central bank rhetoric.

text: with the gains of its policy initiatives over the years as its bedrock we are setting our sights on digital innovations
llm_diagnosis: 'Gains of policy initiatives' and 'setting our sights on digital innovations' is forward-looking boosterism about productivity gains, but Abel has no digital-innovation or productivity node and over-flattened to neutral. The hawkish signal comes from the self-congratulatory progress framing, which the Markov blanket cannot detect.

This is keyword-triggered wrong direction - the FDI language misled it when every growth indicator in the sentence is up.

text: with these developments private sector credit is expected to gain momentum
llm_diagnosis: 'Private sector credit expected to gain momentum' is a textbook hawkish expansion signal, yet Abel's blanket has no explicit credit-aggregate node and neutralized. The causal chain credit growth to GDP to inflation pressure requires an extra link Abel's four-node graph does not encode.

It missed that central bank commentary praising intangibles/R&D capacity carries an implicit supply-side optimism coded hawkish in this dataset.
